mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing FEDFUNDS change and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive FEDFUNDS trends coincide with negative forward returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it crosses a threshold historically associated with the Sahm rule recession signal, triggering rapid risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings deterioration.
failure_condition: Following triggered instances, SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the effect size is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal robust consumer and investment demand that is not decomposable into either series alone; the co-movement indicates broad-based economic strength that supports corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances fail to outperform single-series-only trigger instances by a statistically significant margin, or 60-day forward returns following joint triggers are negative in more than 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth trends predict forward equity returns only during monetary tightening regimes (FEDFUNDS above 4%), where positive INDPRO momentum signals resilience against tightening headwinds and drives outperformance; during easing regimes the relationship is muted because valuations are already supported by falling rates.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 20d forward returns during FEDFUNDS > 4% regimes is not meaningfully higher (by more than 0.02) than during FEDFUNDS <= 4% regimes, rejecting the regime-conditional claim.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI print that exceeds the trailing 12-month average pace by more than 0.2 percentage points signals an inflation surprise that raises expected future rate hikes, compressing near-term equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM change exceeds trailing 12-month average MoM change by > 0.2pp
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following triggered inflation surprises are positive in more than 55% of instances, or effect is not significant at p < 0.10 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth (PAYEMS) deceleration combined with a simultaneous uptick in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly indicates a genuine labor market inflection rather than noisy monthly variation; this combined signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following month as markets price in slower consumption and earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change turns negative AND UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint labor deterioration signal fails to produce negative 20d forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal underperforms either single-series signal alone.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained trend growth in housing starts (HOUST) over the trailing 6 months acts as a leading indicator of broader construction and durable-goods-linked economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as the effect propagates through supply chains and employment.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 6-month HOUST growth and 60d forward returns remains below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows, or the relationship reverses sign in more than one of the last three economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: The real policy rate, approximated by the gap between FEDFUNDS and trailing CPIAUCSL inflation, predicts forward equity returns conditionally on regime: in a high real-rate regime (real rate above 1.5%), further real rate increases restrict financial conditions and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter, while in a negative real-rate regime the relationship is insignificant.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate > 1.5%
failure_condition: In periods where the real rate exceeds 1.5%, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the conditional effect is not statistically distinguishable from the unconditional baseline (p > 0.10).
